---
title: "Case 3: $J/\\psi\\to \\eta\\, p\\bar p$"
---

**Status:** <span class="badge-open">to do</span> — designated clean
playground.

## Why this case matters

Strong (OZI-suppressed but strong/EM) charmonium decay: **C and P are both
exact**, the initial state is a C and P eigenstate
($J^{PC}=1^{--}$), and the final state $\eta\,p\bar p$ is globally
self-conjugate. Unlike $B_s^0\to J/\psi\,p\bar p$ there is no weak-decay
caveat: every good symmetry is available, so every derived relation can be
tested in its exact form. Whatever recipe works here transfers to the $B_s$
case with the "effective C" restriction.

## Setting

$J/\psi(0)\to \eta(1)\, p(2)\, \bar p(3)$. Chains:

- $N^*\to \eta p$ (pair 12, spectator $\bar p$) and conjugate
  $\bar N^*\to\eta\bar p$ (pair 31, spectator $p$),
- $X\to p\bar p$ (pair 23, spectator $\eta$), with $X$ self-conjugate
  mesonic resonances; C-parity of each $X$ constrains which chains couple.

## Constraints to derive

1. **C:** relates $g(N^*\bar p)$ to $g(\bar N^* p)$ chain by chain, at same
   momenta and helicities (fixed frame), with phases
   $\eta_C^{(J/\psi)}\eta_C^{(\eta)}\eta_C^{(p\bar p\ \text{pair})}$ — the
   fermion-pair C phase requires care (fermion–antifermion exchange +
   $(-1)^{L+S}$-type factors for the $p\bar p$ system in the $X$ chains).
2. **P:** Jacob–Wick parity relations at each vertex — standard.
3. **Combined:** for the $X\to p\bar p$ chains, C and P together reproduce
   the known selection rules ($J^{PC}$ of $p\bar p$ levels:
   $C=(-1)^{L+S}$, $P=(-1)^{L+1}$) — a built-in sanity check of the phase
   conventions, since these selection rules are convention-free.
4. **Baryon-antibaryon helicity conventions:** the $v$-spinor phase enters
   the tensor bridge here for the first time in the case-study sequence.

## Worked plan

1. Fixed-frame amplitude with all three chain families; impose C via
   @eq-c-master.
2. Derive coupling relations incl. alignment phases (the $N^*$ vs $\bar N^*$
   chains have mirrored kinematics — same machinery as the kaon swap in
   case 2, but now the *swapped* particles are the fermions).
3. Check against the convention-free $p\bar p$ selection rules.
4. Tensor bridge with explicit $u$, $v$ spinors.
5. Distill: this fixes Recipe C steps 4–5 and ledger item L6 (parity phase).

## Relation to case 4

The result should reduce to the $B_s^0\to J/\psi\, p\bar p$ symmetrization
when the exact C constraint is restricted to the gluon-produced $p\bar p$
subsystem. Keep the derivation parallel.
